metadata.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

transcript.text: Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. All right. All right. All right. It is 11 31 local times, so we will go ahead and get started. This is the NTP working group at IATF125. So remind you, this session will be recorded. This is the IATF note well. You have agreed to this when you registered for this meeting or by joining any of our mailing list. We operate. Please take time to read it. If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to the working group chairs or the area directors. Speaking of area directors, we have this is the March meeting. This is the point where we sometimes have a change in leadership. We are going to say goodbye to Eric, our long serving and hardworking AD and Eric, did you want to mention the incoming AD? Yes, sorry, sorry. Thank you for bearing with me for six years. It has been very interesting to be your area director and to try to help get as much public as possible. Taking over from for me tomorrow is Tommy Jensen who will be with the incoming in-day days. But Tommy say hello and let me also just say thank you to everybody for her being with. Thank you Tommy. I have big shoes to fill. Hello everyone I'm Tommy Jensen. I'm going to be taking over as you responsible area director Sergeant Tomorrow. fingers crossed after you've successfully cleared all the discusses. I keep them. I'll be one left. I look forward to ensuring that you can keep your work unlocked and otherwise that I stay had a real way. Looking forward to looking at you. Thank you again thank you very much to Eric for all of his support over these years and we look forward to working with Tommy going forward. For this meeting I am in Shenzhen and Ditor is at home. So with that, the next couple slides are the meeting tips and resources. We have a what looks like a long agenda but it'll be pretty brief because it's a lot of this is going to be just quick updates on documents. We have the updates on the current working documents. Then we also have some individual submissions and we have a quick IEEE 1588 update. There's two documents that are beyond the working group. The NTP for NTP document is in the RSE editor queue and so we hope that it will be passed soon. I forgot my I can't find my tab where I was cheating ahead of the list. I would speak. The second document is the one that Tommy just alluded to which is the rough time document that is proceeding. It has gone past IES yet. It's gone past the IETF last call. It's gone through IESG. They are in the process of clearing the discusses on that. There's been an enormous amount of work over the last few weeks. Authors, thank you. You've been very responsive and I think we are pretty close to being able to clear that before Eric Leats. I don't think there's anything more to add on that. Does anybody have any questions on either of those two documents? We're down to one discuss. That moves us on to the next set of the actual working group documents that are still within our working group. The first one of these is the NTP V5 document. There has been a recent update published for that. And along with that, there's a list of the four remaining issues that Mary Slav has identified that have been sent to the mailing list. Mary Slav will not be on this meeting. I direct people to the mailing list and his email that has the four issues. We've also had a really nice review recently. We really really need to get some additional reviews on that document. But it is coming into shape. If there are any additional comments on that, please feel free to join the queue or I don't think Mary Slav is not coming. I don't think TELL was coming either. Yeah, TELL is not on the list. So that documents making good progress. We will schedule a virtual interim in between now in Vienna. And then we hope to have a hackathon project in Vienna around that document. The second document that is working group document that we're working on is the NTS for PTP document. I know Martin is on the call. Martin, do you want to give a quick update on that? Yes, of course. So, yeah, there has been a lot of progress at the last meeting. So the NTS for PTP was almost finished. Essentially, only sex likes, QT considerations and other considerations as the missing. Why at least not complete? A few smaller adjustments as the pending and the depends on the current developments in the ITB security subcommittee. But I expect that wine and I will be able to finalize NTS for PTP draft in the upcoming month. I will also integrate and test the upcoming draft changes in the current NTS for PTP implementation. If everything works fine, then I guess the draft is fine or should be fine. So hopefully this year, we can say the staff is fine. So this is my hope. And thank you Eric for the last year, the last year. So yeah, please come to state. I've mentioned this in previous NTP calls, but the course-planing document is making progress to the IEEE 1588 security subcommittee. If there's anybody who needs access to that document for the purposes of review, please reach out to me. And a lot of progress has been made. There was a face to face in December for that committee. And there's been a lot of progress made since then. So hopefully that document is also getting pretty close. The third document that we have is the NTS R&TP Pools document. We have adopted that as a working group document. We still need to get a document submitted as a working group document, but it has been approved. This soon is the next version is submitted with the name as a working group document. We have adopted this under the as an experimental RFC with the intent of primarily documenting the current experiment that's ongoing additional proposals for ways to do NTS Pools are welcome in additional drafts, but that's not what the point of this one will be. And David, did you want to say anything about that document? Schedule for that one. No, I think you said it's fine. So that brings us to the end of the working group document segment. The individual submissions segment. The first document I wanted to discuss was the NTPP V5 Algorithm document. I have had a conversation with Sarah Grant offline about this document. The feedback from our last virtual interim is that it's been it's too early for this document. And so her decision is to let this one expire and then we'll revisit it later when it's a little bit further along. Like people to continue thinking about. Because in NTPV5, we've separated the protocol from the algorithms unlike NTPV4, what material do we need to have published around algorithms and what's the proper time frame for that material? Are there any questions about that? No. The time stamp extension field. We've had no further progress on. And the DNS record document. We also discussed at the virtual interim and there was a desired continuation of the conversation on the mailing list. So that brings us to the last individual submission which is the hardware rooted attestation for PTP. Okay. Diego. So I hope I will be brief as well. Not as Dr. Go against the expert for the fitting. Basically this is a result of some work that we have been making in using PTP4. Something that is not regarding time but space is about using PTP2 to assess the physical location of our servers and it's very much connected with the geolocation attestation and when doing this we have found that so well it would be probably it could be interesting to enhance PTP3's worthiness by providing mechanisms for attesting the messages and so being able to evaluate how the slaves is not slaves. It is essential to have the slaves because it was the time has been assigned from in a PTP network and having a mechanism that can allow you to trace back how this happening and which has been the message. The messages has been received and to the services of the process but without impacting the impact in the protocol itself in the time sense activity. So the idea is whether it is the non-reboedation problem without impacting the throughput and trying to address the extension to use post quantum cryptography. Basically that using and this idea of using this physical location using the TPM. So roughly speaking is something about assuming that we have a hardware based route of trust that can be used as the base for the whole trust fabric and applying the same mechanisms that are already applying in groups like whimsy using this specific tool to issue very far-all ideas that can be used for the attestation. Let me say this is a lot of testing the protocol. It is not about changing the protocol by any means. It is about making an additional mechanism so we can remain sure that all the time processing has been done properly. The third tire is about what is called as a multi-sexyq-shuse instead of individually signing each one of the messages in the S2 build a package of batches of these messages and using a micro-true to ensure the integrity of these batches. Basically that we are proposing is that all the chain of time updates is built by providing this operational keys that are distributed from the hardware route of trust and the guarantee that there is no impact in the signature verification that the impact is is mimized by the fact that we are dealing with batches of those messages. These were the little as well for things we are thinking about using mechanisms that are well-established in software and that well-establishing in the hardware route to move towards PEC-REDNS as is required. With this the idea that as I said this is originates for an ongoing work in rats about physical location attestation, how you can build a proofs that the particular piece of hardware or a particular software is being executed in a particular geographical region. The idea is to properly align what is requested here. The station here so we can link the work and prepare some prototype validation. We have started with an implementation of this geofencing mechanism that is running right now in our labs in Madrid and the idea is to start working on this concrete idea and trying to bring it as a demonstration probably in the next hackathon. The idea of which PEC-REDNS is going to apply and how to apply it in the CBO mechanisms for signing etc. is something that will be a force and we have something that is minimum of the creative draft and the text of the rough is clear because I acknowledge that it is a little bit hard to follow right now but the idea would be to adopt to look for an option with this idea and let me say it is not a change for the protocol, it is not a change for the PTP protocol is a change I mean it is adding the value that you can attest the the results of the use of PTP. That is all. All right, are there any questions? Okay. You don't have any questions right, darling? Well, the one thing I mean I happened to chair the 1588 security working group so I'd like to get their feedback on this. If it is no changes to PTP. No, it is not change and it is trying to bring the or somehow breach between these and what rats is doing in a demonstration. Let's see. I think what I'd like to understand better is how this fits into other related work in the ITF. Sure. Because it is a little bit outside of it. Yeah, no, we were discussing where to bring these because for rats is our, no, no, we want to just PTP because it is the most critical in terms of the or the handling in terms of of tight secretization. And for rats is probably too much, time oriented for I know that for NTP is probably too much at the station oriented. To find the balance. Yeah, now it I definitely appreciate you bringing it here. And I think we'll have to have a conversation about what to do with it. And follow up from there. Oh, go ahead, Eric. You know, I was just going to offer that something the incoming AD can do is, you know, help you socialize the topic with security ADs and stuff that they say because it sounds like. Yeah, I want to take a run through a sag or something else. Yeah, that makes perfect sense. You run it through sag with the other related. I mean, you'd be sure that we have the connectivity to the other related activities. As a standalone document is like, wow, why is the NTP working you're doing this? But if there's two or three documents, I'd like to know what the connectivity is to other documents being progressed in other working groups. OK, so the next item, is there any other questions on this document? Nope. The, uh, I wait to have to take this later. OK. The last item is a quick 1588 working group update. The work on CSPTP is continuing. So that's the client server version of PTP. There, there is a architectural decision on one step versus two step clocks that is sort of blocking most of the work at the moment. And as soon as that's resolved, then we expect the work that David has done to roll into, to be updated and then rolled into the course specification for that. And that will be NTS for CSPTP. And then we've already discussed the NTS for PTP work progressing. Other than that, there's no real updates from 1588. They are hoping to have a face-to-face meeting in June that should be confirmed in the next week or two as to whether or not that will proceed as a face-to-face meeting. The other items is we will plan for a virtual interim sometime between now and Vienna. And we would like to have NTP V5 and the NTS for PTP or CSPTP types of hackathon projects in that timeframe, if at all possible. The next item, meeting is Vienna and the one after that is San Francisco. And so the sweet spot for doing hackathon activities with the most participants for us, given our regular participant people, is going to be this in Vienna. So with that, I said it would be a quick meeting. Is there any other comments or questions? Nope, all right. Well, thank you and everybody have a lovely day. I'll leave you a comment.                        Only very personally.
